nativeRange.habitat: Hill streams and clear, fast-moving waters across much of its range, though the species tends to occupy calmer stretches, eddies, and surface-adjacent zones rather than the strongest current.

diet.notes: Accepts most dry foods including flakes and micropellets. Benefits from regular supplementation with small live or frozen foods such as daphnia, brine shrimp, and bloodworm.

summary: The Pearl Danio is a small, iridescent schooling fish native to hill streams across Southeast Asia, prized for its peaceful temperament and tolerance of cooler water. It is generally considered one of the more beginner-friendly danios, well suited to unheated setups, and does best when kept in groups of six or more in a covered tank.
careNotes: Pearl Danios tend to occupy the upper water column and have been documented as accomplished jumpers — a tight-fitting lid is strongly advised. They are generally considered one of the hardier and more beginner-friendly danios, tolerating a wide range of water parameters. The species is notably cold-tolerant (down to around 18°C), making it suitable for cooler, temperate-style setups without a heater in many climates. Males display intensified iridescent coloration during social competition, though serious aggression among conspecifics is rarely reported. The common name 'Pearl' refers to the iridescent silver sheen of the body. Cross-reference: Celestial Pearl Danio (Danio margaritatus) is an unrelated smaller species despite the similar trade name.
breedingNotes: Egg scatterers with no parental care. Spawning has been reported to be triggered by slight temperature drops mimicking the rainy season, combined with conditioning on live or frozen foods. Eggs typically hatch within 24–36 hours. Adults will readily consume eggs and fry, so moving eggs to a separate rearing vessel is generally advised. Breeding is considered relatively straightforward by danio standards.

primary.name: SeriouslyFish
primary.notes: Used as primary after Aquarium Co-Op returned 404 for this species. SeriouslyFish provides comprehensive species profile with tank dimensions, water parameters, and behavioural notes.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked scientific name (Danio albolineatus, family Danionidae), native range (Myanmar to Laos and Sumatra), temperature 20–25°C, pH 6.0–8.0. FishBase lists a max TL of 9.6 cm (likely wild record outlier); aquarium size consistently reported at 5–6 cm across all hobby sources.

additional.name: TFH Magazine
additional.notes: Cross-checked temperature (20–25°C), adult size (5–6.4 cm), tank minimum (20-gallon long = ~76L), escape risk (high jumper), swim zone (upper column). Noted family as Cyprinidae — Danionidae used here per FishBase current taxonomy.

additional.name: Fish Laboratory
additional.notes: Cross-checked lifespan (3–5 years), gH (5–19 dGH), adult size (up to 5 cm), temperature (20–26°C), pH (6.5–7.5 optimal). Provided kH omitted; used controller-supplied range of 3–12.

fish.breedingNotes: The Pearl Danio is an egg scatterer that tends to spawn readily in mature planted tanks when conditioned on live or frozen foods. Adults will consume eggs if left in the tank, so removing them after spawning — or using a spawning grate — is generally advised. Eggs typically hatch within 24 to 36 hours.
